div.h1: Anatomy of a Cache Line (64 bytes)
div.p: The fundamental unit of data transfer between CPU and memory

g.text: Spatial Locality: Subsequent Accesses Hit Same Line
16 reads for price of 1 | 16x amortized benefit

ul.li: Tag bits: Identify which memory block
Set index: Which cache set (for set-associative caches)
Block offset: Which byte within the 64-byte line (6 bits: log₂(64)) Example: Accessing address 0x1004
The int at 0x1004 is at offset 4 in that line

div.p: read int at addr 0x1000 (4 bytes needed)
All 64 bytes now cached int returned in 4 cycles
